expectations.text: References the Quran Foundation User API (apis.quran.foundation or apis-prelive.quran.foundation)
expectations.evidence: Mentions the QF User API client and existing integration in quran-user-api.ts

expectations.text: Explains the OAuth2 authentication requirement (PKCE flow or client credentials)
expectations.evidence: Identifies existing OAuth2 PKCE auth in auth.ts and references it for the sync feature

expectations.text: Mentions relevant user API endpoints (activity-days, bookmarks, collections, or reading-sessions)
expectations.evidence: Proposes using the Collections API (/auth/v1/collections) for storing memorized surah data

expectations.text: Suggests a local-first architecture with optional cloud sync
expectations.evidence: Proposes local IndexedDB tracking with optional QF Collections API sync for cross-device persistence

expectations.text: Provides a coherent architectural plan with specific data model suggestions
expectations.evidence: Provides SurahHifzRecord type, three-phase implementation plan, identifies 8 files to modify
